suggestion_reasons.Reader-Centric Framing: The rubric-guided draft scored highest and the user confirmed this criterion is working — the engineering-lead framing ('their sequencing is now wrong, not just FYI things changed') is exactly what this criterion targets. Kept unchanged.
suggestion_reasons.Action-Oriented Endings: The rubric-guided draft scored highest on this criterion and the user's comments confirm the closing behavior they want — no trailing sentiment, forward motion only. Kept unchanged.
suggestion_reasons.Deliberate Structural Arc: The user explicitly called out that the generic draft 'leads with the news before I've even registered the situation,' which is precisely what this criterion guards against — and the rubric-guided draft scored highest here. Kept unchanged.
suggestion_reasons.Conciseness: The rubric-guided draft scored highest and the user flagged 'happy to align on that if it doesn't work on your end' and 'flag any dependencies or resource conflicts' as bloat and jargon — both failures this criterion already covers. Kept unchanged.
suggestion_reasons.Tone Calibration: The user called out 'happy to align on that if it doesn't work on your end' as soft and undermining the ask, and 'flag any dependencies or resource conflicts' as jargon-adjacent — added a dimension explicitly flagging hedged softeners and jargon-adjacent phrasing as tone failures, since the existing dimensions didn't surface these patterns strongly enough in scoring.
suggestion_reasons.Clear Ownership in Next Steps: The rubric-guided draft scored highest and the user confirmed the single-owner, specific-deadline structure is exactly right. Kept unchanged.

suggestion_reasons.Reader-Centric Framing: Already the strongest differentiator — the rubric-guided draft scored 5 versus 2 for both others, and the user's commentary explicitly praised this criterion's effect ('centers the reader's situation'). Kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Deliberate Structural Arc: The rubric judge flagged that even the rubric-guided draft missed the grounding acknowledgment beat, and the user confirmed this ('neither A nor C follow the right order'). Strengthened the opening_acknowledgment dimension to make explicit that the grounding beat must reference the reader's existing trajectory or prior commitment — not just generic context — before the news lands.
suggestion_reasons.Conciseness: Scoring strongly and differentiating correctly across all three drafts. Kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Tone Calibration: Scoring correctly and no evidence of misalignment with user preferences. Kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Clear Ownership in Next Steps: Scoring strongly and differentiating correctly — the rubric-guided draft scored 5 versus 1 and 2 for the others. Kept exactly as-is.
